localBiteReport: Reports from the past week out of the Santa Rosa Beach and Destin area of Choctawhatchee Bay show speckled trout as the standout inshore species, with guides describing the trout bite as strong and fish scattered across the bay despite some wind-driven limitations on where you can fish. Redfish are also active — one recent report highlighted a large bull red landed near the bay, and guides note reds are cruising the beaches and bay flats. On the inshore front overall, one Gulf Breeze guide noted the bite had been tough but was improving daily as of early July. Flounder, sheepshead, and black drum are not specifically called out in the most current week's reports, though they remain seasonally present in the bay. Note: the most detailed captain reports are 6–7 days old as of July 9, 2026 — current but at the edge of the weekly reporting cycle.
localBiteSource: Captain Experiences (captainexperiences.com) Choctawhatchee Bay and Destin July 2026 reports; FishingBooker (fishingbooker.com) Choctawhatchee Bay July 2026 reports · Auto-refreshed via Claude API
